LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddler_SalvageTrait_desc: Removes given upgrade from this weapon, it can be reapplied to another unique weapon.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddler_AddTrait_desc: Adds previously salvaged upgrade to this weapon. If you want to replace existing upgrade, remove it first.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddler_WeaponUpgradeNotAvail: No upgrades available for this weapon
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddler_pawnMessedUp: {PAWN_labelShort} has damaged {WEAPON_labelShort} by unskilled tinkering with it.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddler_WeaponUpgrade_Desc: This is a weapon upgrade '{TRAIT_label}', it's category is {CATEGORY}. \nWeapon upgrade allow you to customize unique weapons, adding new features or remove pain points. Applying weapon upgrade is reversible, you can always remove it later. \nMain source of upgrades are salvaged weapons from bygone eras.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddlerSettings_requiresMachi: Applying / Salvaging weapon upgrades requires Machining research to be completed. Default: Yes.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddlerSettings_weaponTraitMa: Maximum amount of unique weapon upgrades:
When adding new weapon upgrades, player can add up to this many upgrades. Does not affect unique weapons generation. Default is 3.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddlerSettings_personaWeapon: Persona weapons can have upgrades salvaged and upgrades applied. Default: Yes.
Applying / Salvaging weapon upgrades from persona weapons requires Machine Persuasion research to be completed. Note: this stacks with required machining research option. Default: Yes.
Maximum amount of persona weapon upgrades:
When adding new weapon upgrades, player can add up to this many upgrades. Does not affect persona weapons generation. Default is 2.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddlerSettings_weaponCanBeDa: Weapon can be damaged by unskilled fiddling
Pawns with low skill have chance to damage weapon when applying or salvaging upgrade. Default: Yes.
